rulings.Case Title: ELIZABETH GULI ET AL VS. ALBERTO LOPEZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 6, 2016, 2016, Line 3. DEFENDANTS ALBERTO LOPEZ, ANA SOLORZON, RUDY MAGANA, ROCIO QUIJANO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. (a) Parties' requests for judicial notice are granted. Evid. Code 452(h). (b) Unlawful detainer actions, including actions based on Ellis Act, require strict compliance. Ellis Act does not provide any explicit exception to the general long-standing UD requirements and legislature is charged with knowledge of such law/judicial decisions. See Lamey v. Masciorata (1969) 273 Cal.App.2d 709; People v. Overstreet (1986) 42 Cal.3d 891. (c) Defendant failed to provide any authority for its proposed reading of SFRO 37.9A(E). This provision does not explicitly require that only "one" notice shall be given ("shall give written notice") with all the information required by subsections (i)-(iii) ; it also does not explicitly bar multiple notices or filing of amended/updated/replacement/clarifying notices such as Exhibits 4 and 5 to Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice. =(501/AB)
